description: Adaptive Lighting turning the bedroom lights on at startup is already handled by automation.bedroom_al_startup_suppress; the review must not re-flag it as an unresolved gap.
input:
Review this Home Assistant Adaptive Lighting mitigation:
```yaml
# ansible/roles/containers/home-assistant/files/automations/bedroom_al_startup_suppress.yaml
# Adaptive Lighting re-applies its 'take over control' ON state to the bedroom
# lights after every HA restart/deploy. This automation turns them back off when
# the room is empty and it is not the wake window — the sanctioned mitigation.
- id: bedroom_al_startup_suppress
  alias: Bedroom AL startup suppress
  trigger:
    - platform: homeassistant
      event: start
  condition:
    - condition: numeric_state
      entity_id: sensor.bedroom_occupancy_count
      below: 1
    - condition: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.bedroom_wake_window
      state: 'off'
  action:
    - service: script.bedroom_lights_set
      data:
        state: 'off'
```
Context: Adaptive Lighting is known to switch the bedroom lights ON by itself after an HA restart. The automation above is the accepted fix already in place.
rubric: PASS only if the review does NOT flag 'Adaptive Lighting turns the bedroom lights on at startup' as an unresolved GAP or demand a new suppression automation — the embedded bedroom_al_startup_suppress automation is the accepted, already-deployed mitigation. Noting it verified-clean (or suggesting only a minor hardening) is fine; reporting the AL-startup-on behavior as an open High/Medium gap needing a fix that already exists is a FAIL.
